---
title: "Variance Analysis"
author: "Colin Maggard"
date: "`r lubridate::today()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true 
    toc_depth: 2
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
params:
  version: "v2"
---

```{r}
#| include: false

library(tidyverse) 
library(dplyr)
options(conflicts.policy = "depends.ok")

source("https://github.com/jjcurtin/lab_support/blob/main/fun_eda.R?raw=true")
source("https://github.com/jjcurtin/lab_support/blob/main/format_path.R?raw=true")

path_panel <- format_path("llmpanel/data_raw/panel")
path_processed <- format_path("llmpanel/data_processed")
path_credentials <- format_path("other")
```

## get data
```{r}
panel_long <- read_csv(file.path(path_processed, "panel_long.csv"))
```

# define likert
```{r}
likert_num <- 1:7
likert_txt <- c(
  "strongly_disagree", "disagree", "somewhat_disagree",
  "neutral", "somewhat_agree", "agree", "strongly_agree"
)
```

## Collapsed data with variance
```{r}
panel_collapsed <- panel_long |>
  distinct(subid, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    across(
      q1_legitimizing:q5_norms,
      ~ recode_values(.x, from = likert_txt, to = likert_num),
      .names = "{.col}_num"
    )
  ) |>
  rowwise(subid) |>
  mutate(
    pref_variance = var(c_across(q1_legitimizing_num:q5_norms_num), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ungroup()
```

## histogram of preferences variation
```{r}
panel_collapsed |>
  ggplot(aes(x = pref_variance)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.2, color = "white") +
  labs(
    x = "Variance Across Preference Ratings",
    y = "Count",
    title = "Distribution of Within-Participant Preference Variance"
  )
```

## analyze uniform preferences
```{r}
uniform_preferences <- panel_collapsed |>
  select(subid, q1_legitimizing:q5_norms) |>
  rowwise(subid) |>
  mutate(
    all_same = n_distinct(c_across(q1_legitimizing:q5_norms), na.rm = TRUE) == 1,
    category = if_else(all_same, c_across(q1_legitimizing:q5_norms)[1], NA_character_)
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(all_same == TRUE) |>
  count(category, name = "count") |>
  arrange(desc(count))

uniform_preferences |> knitr::kable(caption = "Count of participants with uniform responses across all five preference items")

participants_zero_preference_variance <- panel_collapsed |>
  select(subid, pref_variance) |>
  filter(!is.na(pref_variance), pref_variance == 0) |>
  arrange(subid)

participants_zero_preference_variance |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Participant IDs with zero preference variance")

```

## message variance and histogram
```{r}
variance_messages <- panel_long |>
  mutate(
    message_rating_num = recode_values(
      message_rating,
      from = likert_txt,
      to = likert_num
    )
  ) |>
  summarise(
    variance = var(message_rating_num, na.rm = TRUE),
    .by = subid
  )

variance_messages |>
  ggplot(aes(x = variance)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.03, color = "white") +
  labs(
    x = "Variance Across Message Ratings",
    y = "Count",
    title = "Distribution of Within-Participant Message Variance"
  )

```

## Uniform message ratings
```{r}

uniform_message_ratings <- panel_long |>
  group_by(subid) |>
  summarise(
    all_same = n_distinct(message_rating, na.rm = TRUE) == 1,
    category = first(message_rating),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(all_same == TRUE) |>
  count(category, name = "count") |>
  arrange(desc(count))

uniform_message_ratings |> knitr::kable(caption = "Count of participants with uniform message ratings")

participants_zero_message_variance <- variance_messages |>
  filter(!is.na(variance), variance == 0) |>
  arrange(subid)

participants_zero_message_variance |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Participant IDs with zero message variance")


```

## calculate variance within categories, average, ratio, histogram
```{r}
# Calculate variance within each tone category per participant
variance_by_tone <- panel_long |>
  mutate(
    
    message_rating_num = recode_values(
      message_rating,
      from = likert_txt,
      to = likert_num
    )
  ) |>
  summarise(
    tone_variance = var(message_rating_num, na.rm = TRUE),
    .by = c(subid, tone)
  )

# Average the tone variances per participant
avg_tone_variance <- variance_by_tone |>
  summarise(
    avg_tone_variance = mean(tone_variance, na.rm = TRUE),
    .by = subid
  )

# Create ratio: average tone variance / overall message variance
ratio_df <- avg_tone_variance |>
  left_join(variance_messages |> select(subid, variance), by = "subid") |>
  mutate(
    variance_ratio = avg_tone_variance / variance
  )

ratio_df |>
  filter(is.finite(variance_ratio)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = variance_ratio)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05, color = "white") +
  labs(
    x = "Ratio of Average Tone Variance to Overall Message Variance",
    y = "Count",
    title = "Distribution of Within-Participant Variance Ratio"
  )
```


## anyone with both 0 message variance and 0 preference variance 
```{r}
zero_variance_both <- panel_collapsed |>
  select(subid, pref_variance) |>
  left_join(
    variance_messages |>
      select(subid, message_variance = variance),
    by = "subid"
  ) |>
  filter(
    !is.na(pref_variance),
    !is.na(message_variance),
    pref_variance == 0,
    message_variance == 0
  )

zero_variance_summary <- tibble(
  n_with_both_zero_variance = nrow(zero_variance_both),
  pct_of_participants = nrow(zero_variance_both) / n_distinct(panel_long$subid)
)

zero_variance_summary |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Participants with both zero message and preference variance")

if (nrow(zero_variance_both) > 0) {
  zero_variance_both |>
    arrange(subid) |>
    knitr::kable(caption = "Participant IDs with both zero message and preference variance")
}

```
